name: Kakaako Waterfront Park
tagline: Grassy slopes for rolling, ocean views, and open space for running — a simple, free park locals love.
bestTime: Late afternoon for ocean breezes and sunset views; weekday mornings for fewer people
talkStory: This 35-acre waterfront park was built in the 1990s atop a former municipal landfill, transformed into one of Honolulu's best green spaces. The signature grassy hill where keiki roll endlessly was engineered as a cap over the old fill. The promenade offers unobstructed views of Diamond Head, surf breaks, and passing ships. Locals know it as one of the best kite-flying spots on Oahu when the trades blow.
